title: Badge — Count and Status Label Pill
problem: Clip cards need to display version count, frozen status, and activity state as compact labels without consuming layout space. A shared badge component prevents inconsistent pill styling across the app.
analysis: Small pill component: rounded-full, text-xs, px-2 py-0.5, font-medium. Variants: `default` (bg-elevated, text-secondary), `amber` (amber-tinted bg, amber text), `success` (green-tinted), `danger` (red-tinted). Used for: version count ('v3'), frozen status (🔒 Frozen), processing status, stage chips on clip cards.
successCriteria:
- Renders as a rounded pill at text-xs
- All four variants render with correct color scheme
- Does not break layout when text is long (clips at max-w)
implementation: components/ui/Badge.tsx: props { variant?: 'default'|'amber'|'success'|'danger', children }. Pure presentational — no state.

decisions.decision: Badge is purely presentational — no click handling
decisions.rationale: Interactive badges (e.g., stage chip that opens a picker) are handled by the caller wrapping Badge in a <button>. Badge itself never manages interaction state.
decisions.recommendation: StageSelector wraps Badge in a button; VotePanel uses raw Badge for tallies.

userGuide: Small labels on clip cards show version count, frozen status, and processing state.
